---
title: "term project part 3"
author: "Blau, Gavriel"
date: "2024-08-05"
output:
  pdf_document: default
  html_document: default
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```
```{r}
library(readr)  
library(readxl) 
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(purrr)
library(tidyr)
```
```{r}

gdp_data <- read_csv("C:/Users/chezk/Downloads/Data-Table 1.csv")
green_data <- read_excel("C:/Users/chezk/Downloads/SDG_11-7-1.xlsx")
health_data <- read_csv("C:/Users/chezk/Downloads/PLACES__Local_Data_for_Better_Health__County_Data_2023_release_20240804.csv")
```
```{r}
head(gdp_data)
head(green_data)
head(health_data)

```
```{r}
sum(is.na(gdp_data))
sum(is.na(green_data))
sum(is.na(health_data))


# Remove rows with missing values
gdp_data <- na.omit(gdp_data)
green_data <- na.omit(green_data)
# health_data <- na.omit(health_data)

```
```{r}
sum(duplicated(gdp_data))
sum(duplicated(green_data))
sum(duplicated(health_data))


# Remove duplicate rows
gdp_data <- gdp_data[!duplicated(gdp_data), ]
green_data <- green_data[!duplicated(green_data), ]
# health_data <- health_data[!duplicated(health_data), ]

```
```{r}
head(gdp_data)
head(green_data)
head(health_data)

```
```{r}
# Display a summary of each cleaned dataset
summary(gdp_data)
summary(green_data)
# summary(health_data)

```
```{r}
gdp_data <- gdp_data %>%
  separate(City, into = c('city_name', 'state'), sep = ', ')

green_data_2020 <- green_data %>%
  filter(`Data Reference Year` == 2020)
health_data_2020 <- health_data %>%
  filter(Year == 2020)
```

```{r}
green_data_2020_ <- green_data_2020 %>%
  filter(`Country or Territory Name` == "United States of America")

```
```{r}
gdp_data <- gdp_data %>% rename(location = city_name)
green_data_2020 <- green_data_2020 %>% rename(location = `City Name`)
health_data_2020 <- health_data_2020 %>% rename(location = LocationName)
```
```{r}

green_data_2020 <- green_data_2020 %>% rename(urban_population_access = `Average share of urban population with convenient access to open public spaces (%) [b]`)
```

```{r}
common_locations <- reduce(list(gdp_data, health_data_2020), function(x, y) {
  inner_join(x, y, by = "location")
})
```
```{r}
economy_and_health <- common_locations %>%
  select(location, Gdp, Data_Value)

View(economy_and_health)

```
```{r}
summary_stats <- common_locations %>%
  summarize(mean_col1 = mean(Gdp, na.rm = TRUE),
            mean_col2 = mean(Data_Value, na.rm = TRUE),
  )

print(summary_stats)

```
```{r}
common_locations2 <- reduce(list(green_data_2020, health_data_2020), function(x, y) {
  inner_join(x, y, by = "location")
})

```

```{r}
green_health <- common_locations2 %>%
  select(location, urban_population_access, Data_Value)

View(green_health)
unique_values <- unique(green_health$location)
print(unique_values)
```
```{r}
common_locations3 <- reduce(list(green_data_2020, gdp_data), function(x, y) {
  inner_join(x, y, by = "location")
})

```
```{r}
economy_green <- common_locations3 %>%
  select(location, urban_population_access, Gdp)
View(economy_green)
```
```{r}
ggplot(economy_green, aes(x = Gdp, y = urban_population_access)) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(title = "Gdp vs green",
       x = "Gdp",
       y = "green space")

```
```{r}
ggplot(economy_and_health, aes(x = Gdp, y = Data_Value)) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(title = "Gdp vs Health",
       x = "Gdp",
       y = "average health")
```
```{r}
ggplot(green_health, aes(x = urban_population_access, y = Data_Value)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +  # Adds some transparency to the points
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "blue") +  # Adds a trend line
  labs(
    title = "Green Spaces vs Health",
    x = "Average Share of Urban Population with Access to Open Public Spaces (%)",
    y = "average health"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() 
```
```{r}
# The trend line is nearly horizontal, indicating that there might be little to
# no linear relationship between the "Access to Open Public Spaces" and the 
# "Data Value".
# The slope of the line is close to zero, suggesting that changes in access to
# green spaces do not strongly correlate with changes in the data values in your
# dataset. There is variability in the "Data Value" across all access levels,
# but it does not appear to follow a clear trend that increases or decreases 
# consistently with access to green spaces
```
```{r}
ggplot(green_health, aes(x = urban_population_access, y = Data_Value)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +  # Adds some transparency to the points
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = FALSE, color = "red") +  # Adds a non-linear trend line
  labs(
    title = "Green Space vs Health",
    x = "Average Share of Urban Population with Access to Open Public Spaces (%)",
    y = "average health"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```
```{r}
polynomial_model <- lm(Data_Value ~ poly(urban_population_access, 2), data = green_health)
summary(polynomial_model)
```
```{r}
ggplot(green_health, aes(x = urban_population_access, y = Data_Value)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ poly(x, 2), se = FALSE, color = "blue") +
  labs(title = "Green Space vs Average health",
       x = "Average Share of Urban Population with Access to Open Public Spaces (%)",
       y = "average health")
```
